diagrams.title: 1:N Relationship Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows how a Department-Employee relationship (1:N) is mapped with foreign key in Employee table
elements: Employee table with PK EmpID and FK DeptID
diagrams.purpose: Understand where foreign key is placed in 1:N relationships - always in the 'many' side entity

diagrams.title: M:N Relationship Mapping
diagrams.description: Illustrates Student-Course M:N relationship creating an intersection Enroll table
elements: Enroll table with composite PK (StudentID, CourseID) and Grade attribute
diagrams.purpose: Shows that M:N relationships require a separate intersection relation to resolve the many-to-many cardinality

diagrams.title: Weak Entity Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows Account-Payment weak entity relationship with composite primary key
elements: Payment (weak) with fields PaymentID, Amount, Date and FK AccountNumber
diagrams.purpose: Demonstrate how weak entities depend on owner entity's primary key for their identification

diagrams.title: Ternary Relationship Mapping
diagrams.description: Shows how a ternary relationship (Project-Employee-Department) is mapped with three foreign keys
elements: ProjectAssignment table with three FKs and composite PK (ProjectID, EmpID, DeptID)
diagrams.purpose: Visualize mapping for relationships involving more than two entity types
